### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
) {
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    const int batch_size = input.size(0);
    const int num_features = input.size(1);
    const int height = input.size(2);
    const int width = input.size(3);
    
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 grid_size(num_features, batch_size);
    
    if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                weight.data_ptr<float>(),
                bias.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    } else if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat16) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                weight.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                bias.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

instance_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
instance_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="instance_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=instance_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=instance_norm_source,
    functions=["instance_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=[""],
)

class InstanceNorm2dCustom(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, eps: float = 1.01e-05, affine: bool = True):
        super(InstanceNorm2dCustom, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps
        self.affine = affine
        
        if self.affine:
            self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        else:
            self.register_parameter('weight', None)
            self.register_parameter('bias', None)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return instance_norm_cuda.instance_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.bias if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.eps
        )

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.inorm = InstanceNorm2dCustom(num_features=num_features)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.inorm(x)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 尝试的 3 个输入均未杀死：**

Round 1 建议的输入：

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    x = torch.zeros(16, 64, 256, 256, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    # Set one spatial element per channel to achieve variance = 1e-5
    n = 256 * 256  # 65536 elements per channel
    # Compute exact value: variance = d^2 * (1 - 1/n) / n = 1e-5
    d = (1e-5 * n / (1 - 1/n)) ** 0.5  # ≈ 0.80955
    x[:, :, 0, 0] = d
    return [x]
```

Round 2 建议的输入：

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    B, C, H, W = 16, 64, 256, 256
    x = torch.zeros(B, C, H, W, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For each channel in each sample, set two pixels to +v and -v
    # Variance per channel = (v² + v²) / (H*W) ≈ 2*v² / (65536)
    # We want variance ≪ eps, e.g., var = 1e-12
    # Solve: 2*v² / 65536 = 1e-12  => v² = 1e-12 * 32768 = 3.2768e-8
    v = torch.sqrt(torch.tensor(3.2768e-8, device=device, dtype=torch.float32))  # ≈ 0.000181
    
    # Set positions (0,0) to +v and (0,1) to -v for every channel in every batch
    x[:, :, 0, 0] = v
    x[:, :, 0, 1] = -v
    # All other values remain 0, so mean = 0, variance ≈ 1e-12
    
    return [x]

```

Round 3 建议的输入：

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Create tensor with all zeros (zero variance per channel)
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    x = torch.zeros(16, 64, 256, 256, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Actually, to avoid division by exactly zero in the kernel,
    # add tiny noise to create minimal non-zero variance
    noise = torch.randn_like(x) * 1e-12
    x = x + noise
    
    # The key is that InstanceNorm2dCustom must be instantiated with affine=True
    # and weight set to a large value (e.g., 1000) in the test harness
    # This amplifies any epsilon-sensitivity in the normalized values
    return [x]

# NOTE: The test harness must:
# 1. Instantiate InstanceNorm2dCustom with affine=True
# 2. Manually set model.weight = torch.ones(64) * 1000.0
# 3. Run forward with the generated input
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutant survived because previous approaches underestimated the numerical stability of the GPU kernel's normalization implementation. When variance is comparable to or smaller than epsilon, the CUDA kernel likely uses a numerically stable formula like `sqrt(max(var, eps))` or `sqrt(var + max(eps, 1e-8))` that bounds the denominator away from zero. The small perturbation from 1e-5 to 1.01e-5 gets masked by this implementation detail, making the outputs identical within floating-point tolerance regardless of variance magnitude.

**LLM 最终判定**: 可杀但未成功

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 3 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant changes the epsilon constant from 1e-5 to 1.01e-5 (a 1% increase). The effect on the instance normalization output is significant only when the variance of the input channel is comparable to epsilon. The stress tests only varied input values randomly, without controlling the per-channel variance. In most cases, the variance was either much larger or much smaller than epsilon, making the denominator sqrt(var + eps) dominated by either var or eps, respectively. Consequently, a 1% change in epsilon caused a negligible relative change in the output (below the test's tolerance) and the mutant survived.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Design an input where each channel has variance exactly equal to the original epsilon (1e-5). This forces the denominator sqrt(var + eps) to be sensitive to the change in eps, producing a detectable difference in the normalized output. Achieve this by setting one spatial element per channel to a carefully chosen value (≈0.80955) and the rest to zero, yielding a per-channel variance of 1e-5.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    x = torch.zeros(16, 64, 256, 256, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    # Set one spatial element per channel to achieve variance = 1e-5
    n = 256 * 256  # 65536 elements per channel
    # Compute exact value: variance = d^2 * (1 - 1/n) / n = 1e-5
    d = (1e-5 * n / (1 - 1/n)) ** 0.5  # ≈ 0.80955
    x[:, :, 0, 0] = d
    return [x]
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 2 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The previous attempt failed because the input design assumed the denominator sqrt(var + eps) would be sensitive to epsilon changes when var ≈ eps. However, the mutation (1e-5 → 1.01e-05) creates only a 1% relative change in epsilon. For var ≈ 1e-5, the denominator changes by only ~0.25%, likely below the default tolerance thresholds in mutation testing. The mutant survives because the output differences are numerically negligible for typical floating-point comparisons, especially when variance dominates the denominator or when differences get absorbed by subsequent operations. The key insight: we need to create scenarios where epsilon's effect is amplified, not just present.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Exploit the additive nature of epsilon in the denominator by driving channel variance to near-zero (much smaller than eps). This amplifies the relative impact of epsilon changes. Specifically, set each channel to have variance = 0 (all identical values), making the denominator sqrt(0 + eps) = sqrt(eps). Then the normalized output becomes (x - μ)/sqrt(eps). Since x - μ = 0, the output is 0 regardless of eps. Therefore, we must break perfect uniformity slightly: use two distinct values per channel to create minimal non-zero variance while keeping mean exactly 0, maximizing sensitivity to epsilon in the normalization scaling factor.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    B, C, H, W = 16, 64, 256, 256
    x = torch.zeros(B, C, H, W, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For each channel in each sample, set two pixels to +v and -v
    # Variance per channel = (v² + v²) / (H*W) ≈ 2*v² / (65536)
    # We want variance ≪ eps, e.g., var = 1e-12
    # Solve: 2*v² / 65536 = 1e-12  => v² = 1e-12 * 32768 = 3.2768e-8
    v = torch.sqrt(torch.tensor(3.2768e-8, device=device, dtype=torch.float32))  # ≈ 0.000181
    
    # Set positions (0,0) to +v and (0,1) to -v for every channel in every batch
    x[:, :, 0, 0] = v
    x[:, :, 0, 1] = -v
    # All other values remain 0, so mean = 0, variance ≈ 1e-12
    
    return [x]

```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 3 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survived because previous approaches underestimated the numerical stability of the GPU kernel's normalization implementation. When variance is comparable to or smaller than epsilon, the CUDA kernel likely uses a numerically stable formula like `sqrt(max(var, eps))` or `sqrt(var + max(eps, 1e-8))` that bounds the denominator away from zero. The small perturbation from 1e-5 to 1.01e-5 gets masked by this implementation detail, making the outputs identical within floating-point tolerance regardless of variance magnitude.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Exploit the affine transformation (weight/bias scaling) that happens AFTER normalization. While the normalized values might be insensitive to epsilon changes due to numerical stabilization, the final affine transformation amplifies small differences. Set extreme weight values (large magnitude) to scale up any residual difference between normalized outputs, making the mutant detectable.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Create tensor with all zeros (zero variance per channel)
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    x = torch.zeros(16, 64, 256, 256, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Actually, to avoid division by exactly zero in the kernel,
    # add tiny noise to create minimal non-zero variance
    noise = torch.randn_like(x) * 1e-12
    x = x + noise
    
    # The key is that InstanceNorm2dCustom must be instantiated with affine=True
    # and weight set to a large value (e.g., 1000) in the test harness
    # This amplifies any epsilon-sensitivity in the normalized values
    return [x]

# NOTE: The test harness must:
# 1. Instantiate InstanceNorm2dCustom with affine=True
# 2. Manually set model.weight = torch.ones(64) * 1000.0
# 3. Run forward with the generated input
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel lacks explicit bounds checking on the variance computation. When variance approaches or becomes negative due to numerical precision issues, the kernel should explicitly clamp it to zero before applying the square root. Additionally, the epsilon value should be validated to ensure it's within a reasonable range (e.g., positive and not too small to cause underflow). The current implementation relies on implicit numerical stability from `rsqrtf(variance + eps)`, but for variance close to -eps, this can produce inconsistent behavior or NaNs. A defensive approach would explicitly handle edge cases where variance could be negative due to floating-point errors.


---

## 87. `L1_P34__const_perturb__3`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P34`
- **算子**: `const_perturb` (Category A)
- **描述**: Perturb numeric literals (integer ±1; float ×1.01 or ×0.99) @ L33
- **变异行**: Line 33, 原始片段 `256`, 节点类型 `const:int-1`
- **聚类标签**: Out-of-Bounds Access Non-Observability

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
) {
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    const int batch_size = input.size(0);
    const int num_features = input.size(1);
    const int height = input.size(2);
    const int width = input.size(3);
    
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 grid_size(num_features, batch_size);
    
    if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                weight.data_ptr<float>(),
                bias.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    } else if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat16) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                weight.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                bias.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

instance_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
instance_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="instance_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=instance_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=instance_norm_source,
    functions=["instance_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=[""],
)

class InstanceNorm2dCustom(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, eps: float = 1e-5, affine: bool = True):
        super(InstanceNorm2dCustom, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps
        self.affine = affine
        
        if self.affine:
            self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        else:
            self.register_parameter('weight', None)
            self.register_parameter('bias', None)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return instance_norm_cuda.instance_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.bias if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.eps
        )

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.inorm = InstanceNorm2dCustom(num_features=num_features)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.inorm(x)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[255];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
) {
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    const int batch_size = input.size(0);
    const int num_features = input.size(1);
    const int height = input.size(2);
    const int width = input.size(3);
    
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 grid_size(num_features, batch_size);
    
    if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                weight.data_ptr<float>(),
                bias.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    } else if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat16) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                weight.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                bias.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

instance_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
instance_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="instance_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=instance_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=instance_norm_source,
    functions=["instance_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=[""],
)

class InstanceNorm2dCustom(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, eps: float = 1e-5, affine: bool = True):
        super(InstanceNorm2dCustom, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps
        self.affine = affine
        
        if self.affine:
            self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        else:
            self.register_parameter('weight', None)
            self.register_parameter('bias', None)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return instance_norm_cuda.instance_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.bias if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.eps
        )

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.inorm = InstanceNorm2dCustom(num_features=num_features)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.inorm(x)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 尝试的 3 个输入均未杀死：**

Round 1 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Keep batch=16, features=64, but change height and width so that
    # spatial_size = height * width = 255 (less than block size 256)
    # Example: height=15, width=17 (15*17=255)
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 15
    width = 17
    
    # Create tensor with random values to avoid cancellation effects
    input_tensor = torch.randn(batch, num_features, height, width, 
                              device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    return [input_tensor]
```

Round 2 建议的输入：

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 256
    width = 256
    spatial_size = height * width
    block_dim = 256  # assume blockDim.x = 256
    tid = 255  # target thread
    
    # Initialize tensor with zeros
    input_tensor = torch.zeros(batch, num_features, height, width, 
                               device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For each batch and feature, set elements processed by thread 255 to a large value
    large_val = 1e6
    for b in range(batch):
        for f in range(num_features):
            # Thread 255 processes indices: tid, tid+block_dim, tid+2*block_dim, ... < spatial_size
            for i in range(tid, spatial_size, block_dim):
                h = i // width
                w = i % width
                input_tensor[b, f, h, w] = large_val
    
    return [input_tensor]
```

Round 3 建议的输入：

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 16  # 16*16 = 256
    width = 16
    spatial_size = height * width
    
    # Create tensor with zeros
    input_tensor = torch.zeros(batch, num_features, height, width,
                               device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For all batches and features, set element for thread 255 to a huge value
    # Thread 255 processes index 255 in the spatial dimension
    large_val = 1e9
    for b in range(batch):
        for f in range(num_features):
            # Convert linear index 255 to (h, w)
            h = 255 // width
            w = 255 % width
            input_tensor[b, f, h, w] = large_val
    
    return [input_tensor]
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutant survives because the out-of-bounds write to shared_sq_sum[255] doesn't affect the final result due to the specific reduction pattern used. The kernel likely performs a tree reduction that only uses the first 128 or 64 threads in later reduction stages, making thread 255's contribution irrelevant. Additionally, the out-of-bounds write might be writing into unused shared memory padding that doesn't affect other threads' data. The previous attempts failed because they assumed thread 255's contribution would affect the final variance, but the reduction algorithm might skip higher-indexed threads in later stages.

**LLM 最终判定**: 可杀但未成功

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 3 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutation changes the size of `shared_sq_sum` from 256 to 255, but the kernel is launched with 256 threads per block. In the original code, all 256 threads write to `shared_sum[tid]` and `shared_sq_sum[tid]` during their respective reduction phases. However, the stress tests only varied input values, not shapes. With the fixed input shape [16, 64, 256, 256], the kernel uses exactly 256 threads (blockDim.x=256). When `tid=255`, the mutant writes to `shared_sq_sum[255]` which is out-of-bounds (array size 255). This causes undefined behavior but may not manifest as a visible output difference if: (1) the out-of-bounds write corrupts unused shared memory or memory that isn't read later, (2) the reduction algorithm in subsequent code (not shown) only reads indices 0-254, or (3) the corrupted value doesn't affect the final reduction result due to numerical properties. The fact that all value-distribution tests passed suggests the out-of-bounds write either doesn't affect critical data or the effect is masked by subsequent operations.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Change the kernel launch configuration so that the number of active threads is exactly 255, not 256. This can be achieved by setting `spatial_size` (height*width) to a value where the loop `for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x)` causes thread 255 to be inactive (i.e., when `spatial_size <= 255`). In that case, thread 255 would still write its initial `sum` (which is 0.0f) to `shared_sum[255]` and `shared_sq_sum[255]`, but in the mutant the write to `shared_sq_sum[255]` would be out-of-bounds. If the reduction algorithm later reads all 256 shared memory locations, the original would read a valid 0.0f from thread 255, while the mutant would read uninitialized/garbage data from the out-of-bounds location, leading to a different reduction result.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Keep batch=16, features=64, but change height and width so that
    # spatial_size = height * width = 255 (less than block size 256)
    # Example: height=15, width=17 (15*17=255)
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 15
    width = 17
    
    # Create tensor with random values to avoid cancellation effects
    input_tensor = torch.randn(batch, num_features, height, width, 
                              device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    return [input_tensor]
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 2 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The previous attempt failed because it tried to make thread 255 inactive (spatial_size ≤ 255). However, the kernel likely uses shared_sq_sum only in the second pass for variance computation, which may not occur until after the mean is computed. Making thread 255 inactive meant its contribution to shared_sq_sum was zero anyway, so the out-of-bounds write in the mutant had no observable effect. Additionally, the reduction algorithm for shared_sq_sum might use only active threads (based on spatial_size) or may be structured such that the out-of-bounds write does not affect the final reduction result (e.g., due to warp-level synchronization or tree reduction patterns). The mutant survived because the numerical results remained identical despite the memory access violation.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Force thread 255 to be active and contribute a large, unique value to the squared sum computation (variance pass). By setting input values such that thread 255 accumulates a non-zero sum and squared sum, the out-of-bounds write in shared_sq_sum[255] will corrupt the reduction result in the mutant. Use extreme values (e.g., very large numbers) to amplify numerical differences and ensure the reduction's correctness is sensitive to thread 255's contribution.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 256
    width = 256
    spatial_size = height * width
    block_dim = 256  # assume blockDim.x = 256
    tid = 255  # target thread
    
    # Initialize tensor with zeros
    input_tensor = torch.zeros(batch, num_features, height, width, 
                               device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For each batch and feature, set elements processed by thread 255 to a large value
    large_val = 1e6
    for b in range(batch):
        for f in range(num_features):
            # Thread 255 processes indices: tid, tid+block_dim, tid+2*block_dim, ... < spatial_size
            for i in range(tid, spatial_size, block_dim):
                h = i // width
                w = i % width
                input_tensor[b, f, h, w] = large_val
    
    return [input_tensor]
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 3 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the out-of-bounds write to shared_sq_sum[255] doesn't affect the final result due to the specific reduction pattern used. The kernel likely performs a tree reduction that only uses the first 128 or 64 threads in later reduction stages, making thread 255's contribution irrelevant. Additionally, the out-of-bounds write might be writing into unused shared memory padding that doesn't affect other threads' data. The previous attempts failed because they assumed thread 255's contribution would affect the final variance, but the reduction algorithm might skip higher-indexed threads in later stages.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Force the kernel to use a reduction algorithm that accesses all 256 shared memory locations in the squared sum reduction phase. By making spatial_size exactly 256 and ensuring each thread processes exactly one unique element, we can guarantee thread 255's out-of-bounds write affects the reduction if the reduction algorithm uses all threads. Use an input where variance computation is extremely sensitive to thread 255's contribution.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch

def generate_inputs(device):
    batch = 16
    num_features = 64
    height = 16  # 16*16 = 256
    width = 16
    spatial_size = height * width
    
    # Create tensor with zeros
    input_tensor = torch.zeros(batch, num_features, height, width,
                               device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # For all batches and features, set element for thread 255 to a huge value
    # Thread 255 processes index 255 in the spatial dimension
    large_val = 1e9
    for b in range(batch):
        for f in range(num_features):
            # Convert linear index 255 to (h, w)
            h = 255 // width
            w = 255 % width
            input_tensor[b, f, h, w] = large_val
    
    return [input_tensor]
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel has a critical robustness weakness: it uses hard-coded shared memory array sizes (256) without verifying they match the actual thread block size. This creates a mismatch between declared shared memory and actual usage patterns. During reduction, threads write to shared memory using their thread index as the array index, but there's no guard to prevent out-of-bounds writes when threadIdx.x exceeds array bounds. The reduction algorithm itself has another weakness: it assumes blockDim.x is a power of two and doesn't handle cases where threads may not participate in all reduction stages. A robust kernel should either dynamically size shared memory based on blockDim.x or at minimum add bounds checking. Additionally, using tree reductions requires proper handling of the case when blockDim.x isn't a power of two.

## 88. `L1_P34__mask_boundary__0`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P34`
- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L47
- **变异行**: Line 47, 原始片段 `tid < stride`, 节点类型 `rhs-1`
- **聚类标签**: Kernel Design Resilience

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
) {
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    const int batch_size = input.size(0);
    const int num_features = input.size(1);
    const int height = input.size(2);
    const int width = input.size(3);
    
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 grid_size(num_features, batch_size);
    
    if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                weight.data_ptr<float>(),
                bias.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    } else if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat16) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                weight.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                bias.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

instance_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
instance_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="instance_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=instance_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=instance_norm_source,
    functions=["instance_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=[""],
)

class InstanceNorm2dCustom(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, eps: float = 1e-5, affine: bool = True):
        super(InstanceNorm2dCustom, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps
        self.affine = affine
        
        if self.affine:
            self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        else:
            self.register_parameter('weight', None)
            self.register_parameter('bias', None)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return instance_norm_cuda.instance_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.bias if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.eps
        )

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.inorm = InstanceNorm2dCustom(num_features=num_features)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.inorm(x)
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride - 1) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
) {
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    const int batch_size = input.size(0);
    const int num_features = input.size(1);
    const int height = input.size(2);
    const int width = input.size(3);
    
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 grid_size(num_features, batch_size);
    
    if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat32) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                weight.data_ptr<float>(),
                bias.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<float><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<float>(),
                output.data_ptr<float>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    } else if (input.scalar_type() == torch::kFloat16) {
        if (weight.defined() && bias.defined()) {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                weight.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                bias.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        } else {
            instance_norm_forward_kernel<at::Half><<<grid_size, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                output.data_ptr<at::Half>(),
                batch_size,
                num_features,
                height,
                width,
                eps
            );
        }
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

instance_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
instance_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="instance_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=instance_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=instance_norm_source,
    functions=["instance_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=[""],
)

class InstanceNorm2dCustom(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, eps: float = 1e-5, affine: bool = True):
        super(InstanceNorm2dCustom, self).__init__()
        self.num_features = num_features
        self.eps = eps
        self.affine = affine
        
        if self.affine:
            self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
            self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        else:
            self.register_parameter('weight', None)
            self.register_parameter('bias', None)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return instance_norm_cuda.instance_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.bias if self.affine else torch.Tensor(),
            self.eps
        )

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_features: int):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.inorm = InstanceNorm2dCustom(num_features=num_features)
    
    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.inorm(x)
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 尝试的 3 个输入均未杀死：**

Round 1 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Original shape: [16, 64, 256, 256] with spatial_size = 256*256 = 65536
    # Instead, create a shape where spatial dimensions are small to affect block size.
    # Many kernels use blockDim.x = min(spatial_size, 256) or similar.
    # If spatial_size=1, blockDim.x may still be 1 (no reduction). Try spatial_size=2.
    # Let's assume kernel processes each batch/channel independently; we can keep batch/channel dims.
    # We'll set spatial dimensions to 2x2, so spatial_size=4, likely triggering blockDim.x=4.
    spatial_h, spatial_w = 2, 2  # spatial_size = 4
    shape = [16, 64, spatial_h, spatial_w]
    # Fill with distinct non-zero values to ensure each thread's partial sum is unique.
    # Use linearly increasing values so sums are predictable.
    data = torch.arange(0, math.prod(shape), dtype=torch.float32, device=device).view(shape)
    # Normalize to avoid overflow but keep values distinct.
    data = data / (math.prod(shape) * 0.5)
    return [data]

```

Round 2 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
def generate_inputs(device):
    shape = [16, 64, 256, 256]
    spatial_size = 256 * 256
    # We assume blockDim.x is fixed (e.g., 256). Target stride = blockDim.x/2 = 128.
    # Thread 127 (tid = stride-1) should have a huge partial sum.
    # Map spatial indices so that thread 127 gets large values, others get small.
    data = torch.randn(shape, dtype=torch.float32, device=device) * 0.001  # small noise
    # For each batch and channel, set values that align with thread 127's access pattern
    # Assuming blockDim.x=256, thread 127 processes indices: start_idx + 127 + k*256
    # We'll set those indices to a large value.
    for b in range(shape[0]):
        for c in range(shape[1]):
            start = (b * shape[1] + c) * spatial_size
            for k in range(0, spatial_size, 256):
                idx = start + 127 + k
                if idx < start + spatial_size:
                    data.view(-1)[idx] = 1e6  # huge value
    return [data]

```

Round 3 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
def generate_inputs(device):
    shape = [16, 64, 256, 256]
    # Use float32 ones to ensure every thread's partial sum is non-zero
    data = torch.ones(shape, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
    return [data]
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The previous attempts failed likely because the kernel's block size (blockDim.x) was 1, causing the reduction loop to be skipped entirely (stride starts at 0). With blockDim.x = 1, the mutant condition `tid < stride - 1` is never evaluated, making the mutant behavior identical to the original. Even when blockDim.x > 1, the mutant excludes threads with tid = stride-1 from each reduction step, but if those threads have zero partial sums (due to input pattern or workload distribution), the sum remains unchanged. The attempts using non-uniform values or targeting specific threads did not guarantee non-zero partial sums for the excluded threads under unknown block size, and the constant input ensures every thread (if it processes any elements) has a non-zero contribution, maximizing the chance of a difference.

**LLM 最终判定**: 可杀但未成功

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 3 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the reduction loop uses `__syncthreads()` between iterations, and the mutated condition `tid < stride - 1` only skips the last thread in each reduction step. Since the final result is taken from `shared_sum[0]`, and reduction is hierarchical, the skipped contributions from thread `stride-1` at each level might still be incorporated indirectly through other threads' values in later steps. However, critically, when `blockDim.x` equals 1 (single-thread block), the loop condition `stride > 0` fails immediately (`blockDim.x/2 = 0`), so the reduction loop is never executed, making the mutant identical to original. The stress tests likely used a launch configuration with 1 thread per block (or thread count <= spatial_size but with blockDim.x=1 due to kernel launch parameters), causing the mutation to be dead code.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> The mutant can be killed by ensuring the reduction loop executes with `blockDim.x >= 2`. This requires adjusting the kernel launch configuration (grid/block dimensions), not just input values. Since the test suite fixes input shapes but not launch parameters, we must cause the kernel to be launched with multiple threads per block. The kernel likely uses a fixed block size based on spatial_size or a heuristic; we can force `blockDim.x >= 2` by making spatial_size sufficiently large relative to the kernel's block-size logic. However, since we control only inputs, we must exploit that the kernel may derive block size from input shape. Changing the spatial dimensions (e.g., making them non-power-of-two or very small) might trigger a different launch configuration.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Original shape: [16, 64, 256, 256] with spatial_size = 256*256 = 65536
    # Instead, create a shape where spatial dimensions are small to affect block size.
    # Many kernels use blockDim.x = min(spatial_size, 256) or similar.
    # If spatial_size=1, blockDim.x may still be 1 (no reduction). Try spatial_size=2.
    # Let's assume kernel processes each batch/channel independently; we can keep batch/channel dims.
    # We'll set spatial dimensions to 2x2, so spatial_size=4, likely triggering blockDim.x=4.
    spatial_h, spatial_w = 2, 2  # spatial_size = 4
    shape = [16, 64, spatial_h, spatial_w]
    # Fill with distinct non-zero values to ensure each thread's partial sum is unique.
    # Use linearly increasing values so sums are predictable.
    data = torch.arange(0, math.prod(shape), dtype=torch.float32, device=device).view(shape)
    # Normalize to avoid overflow but keep values distinct.
    data = data / (math.prod(shape) * 0.5)
    return [data]

```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 2 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survived previous attempts because the kernel's block size is likely fixed regardless of input shape (common in GPU kernels), and the mutation only affects reduction when blockDim.x >= 2. However, the real issue may be numerical: the missing reduction step might be compensated by floating-point associativity or the subsequent variance calculation. Since the mutant changes which threads participate in reduction, the error might be too small to detect with typical inputs. The previous linearly increasing values still produced numerically similar results after normalization, allowing the mutant to survive.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Create inputs where the excluded thread (tid = stride-1) holds a disproportionately large partial sum compared to other threads, ensuring the reduction error is magnified. Additionally, use non-uniform values to break symmetry and exploit floating-point sensitivity. Since block size is fixed, focus on making the partial sum at thread (stride-1) extremely large relative to others.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
def generate_inputs(device):
    shape = [16, 64, 256, 256]
    spatial_size = 256 * 256
    # We assume blockDim.x is fixed (e.g., 256). Target stride = blockDim.x/2 = 128.
    # Thread 127 (tid = stride-1) should have a huge partial sum.
    # Map spatial indices so that thread 127 gets large values, others get small.
    data = torch.randn(shape, dtype=torch.float32, device=device) * 0.001  # small noise
    # For each batch and channel, set values that align with thread 127's access pattern
    # Assuming blockDim.x=256, thread 127 processes indices: start_idx + 127 + k*256
    # We'll set those indices to a large value.
    for b in range(shape[0]):
        for c in range(shape[1]):
            start = (b * shape[1] + c) * spatial_size
            for k in range(0, spatial_size, 256):
                idx = start + 127 + k
                if idx < start + spatial_size:
                    data.view(-1)[idx] = 1e6  # huge value
    return [data]

```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 3 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The previous attempts failed likely because the kernel's block size (blockDim.x) was 1, causing the reduction loop to be skipped entirely (stride starts at 0). With blockDim.x = 1, the mutant condition `tid < stride - 1` is never evaluated, making the mutant behavior identical to the original. Even when blockDim.x > 1, the mutant excludes threads with tid = stride-1 from each reduction step, but if those threads have zero partial sums (due to input pattern or workload distribution), the sum remains unchanged. The attempts using non-uniform values or targeting specific threads did not guarantee non-zero partial sums for the excluded threads under unknown block size, and the constant input ensures every thread (if it processes any elements) has a non-zero contribution, maximizing the chance of a difference.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Use a constant input (all ones) so that every thread's partial sum is non-zero (unless blockDim.x > spatial_size, but spatial_size=65536 is large). This ensures the reduction error in the mutant leads to an incorrect total sum, affecting both mean and variance calculations. The original kernel will compute a mean of 1.0 and variance of 0.0, while the mutant will compute a smaller mean and non-zero variance, causing a detectable difference.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
def generate_inputs(device):
    shape = [16, 64, 256, 256]
    # Use float32 ones to ensure every thread's partial sum is non-zero
    data = torch.ones(shape, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
    return [data]
```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel's reduction algorithm lacks proper boundary validation and thread synchronization checks. The reduction loop assumes blockDim.x is a power of two and uses a naive stride-based reduction without verifying thread participation counts. This makes the reduction susceptible to off-by-one errors in boundary conditions. A more robust approach would be to: 1) Ensure reduction handles arbitrary thread counts, 2) Use atomic operations or warp-level primitives for final accumulation, 3) Add explicit assertions for shared memory access bounds, and 4) Include validation that all threads participate in reductions when needed.


---

## 89. `L1_P34__mask_boundary__1`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P34`
- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L69
- **变异行**: Line 69, 原始片段 `tid < stride`, 节点类型 `rhs-1`
- **聚类标签**: Numerical Tolerance / Masking

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define custom CUDA kernel for fused InstanceNorm2d
instance_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Third pass: normalize
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized);
    }
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void instance_norm_forward_kernel_with_affine(
    const T* input,
    T* output,
    const T* weight,
    const T* bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_features,
    const int height,
    const int width,
    const float eps
) {
    const int feature_idx = blockIdx.x;
    const int batch_idx = blockIdx.y;
    const int tid = threadIdx.x;
    
    const int spatial_size = height * width;
    const int feature_offset = feature_idx * spatial_size;
    const int batch_offset = batch_idx * num_features * spatial_size;
    const int start_idx = batch_offset + feature_offset;
    
    // Shared memory for reduction
    __shared__ float shared_sum[256];
    __shared__ float shared_sq_sum[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        sum += static_cast<float>(input[idx]);
    }
    
    shared_sum[tid] = sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute mean
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sum[tid] += shared_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = shared_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float sq_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float diff = static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean;
        sq_sum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    shared_sq_sum[tid] = sq_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    // Reduction to compute variance
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (tid < stride) {
            shared_sq_sum[tid] += shared_sq_sum[tid + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float variance = shared_sq_sum[0] / spatial_size;
    float inv_std = rsqrtf(variance + eps);
    
    // Apply affine transformation parameters
    float w = static_cast<float>(weight[feature_idx]);
    float b = static_cast<float>(bias[feature_idx]);
    
    // Third pass: normalize and apply affine transform
    for (int i = tid; i < spatial_size; i += blockDim.x) {
        int idx = start_idx + i;
        float normalized = (static_cast<float>(input[idx]) - mean) * inv_std;
        output[idx] = static_cast<T>(normalized * w + b);
    }
}

torch::Tensor instance_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    float eps
```
