```python
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.z) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 尝试的 2 个输入均未杀死：**

Round 1 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    # Use 16 batches, 64 channels, spatial size 1x1 to make group_elements = channels_per_group
    # Assuming the group normalization uses a group size of 32 (common), each group has 32 elements.
    # The kernel likely launches with a 1D block of 256 threads.
    # Original stride = 256 → only thread 0 processes the first element, others skip.
    # Mutant stride = 1 → all threads process all elements, causing race conditions.
    input_tensor = torch.randn(16, 64, 1, 1, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    return [input_tensor]

```

Round 2 建议的输入：

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Use a non-power-of-two spatial size to avoid alignment
    # batch=1, channels=32, height=7, width=7
    # Group normalization with groups=8 -> 4 channels per group
    # group_elements = 4 * 7 * 7 = 196
    # Launch with 1D block of 128 threads (common but arbitrary)
    # weight and bias are non-null
    input_tensor = torch.randn(1, 32, 7, 7, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    # Return ONLY the input tensor as per specification
    return [input_tensor]

```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutant survives because the mutation (blockDim.x → blockDim.z) doesn't change the kernel's output when the launch configuration uses a 1D block where blockDim.z = 1, which is typical. In this case, the mutant's loop stride becomes 1, causing every thread to process all group elements. However, because the computation for each output element is deterministic and independent of thread ID (same input, mean, inv_std, weight, bias per channel), all threads write identical values to each output index. Even with concurrent writes, the final value remains the same as with the original's partitioned work. The race condition is benign due to idempotent writes. Previous attempts failed because they assumed race conditions would produce different outputs, but the writes are idempotent when weight/bias are per-channel and the same across threads.

**LLM 最终判定**: 不可杀 (equivalent/unkillable)

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 3 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the kernel's launch configuration likely uses a 1D thread block (or a block where blockDim.z = 1). In such configurations, blockDim.x and blockDim.z are equal (both 1 for the z-dimension), making the mutated loop stride identical to the original. Even if blockDim.x > 1, if blockDim.z = 1, the mutated stride becomes 1, which can cause overlapping iterations and race conditions. However, the stress tests kept input shapes fixed, so the launch configuration remained unchanged, and the race condition may have been non‑detectable due to the specific combination of group_elements and block size (e.g., each thread still processes the same set of elements, or the overlapping writes happen to produce the same final output because of the order of writes).

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Change the input shape to force a different launch configuration where blockDim.z is not equal to blockDim.x, or to make group_elements small enough that the difference in stride causes a clear divergence in which threads process which elements. Specifically, reducing the spatial dimensions to 1x1 will make group_elements equal to the number of channels per group. With a typical 1D block of size 256, the original stride (blockDim.x = 256) will cause only the first few threads to process elements (or each thread to process at most one element), while the mutant stride (blockDim.z = 1) will cause every thread to iterate over many elements, leading to overlapping writes and incorrect outputs.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Shape: [batch, channels, height, width]
    # Use 16 batches, 64 channels, spatial size 1x1 to make group_elements = channels_per_group
    # Assuming the group normalization uses a group size of 32 (common), each group has 32 elements.
    # The kernel likely launches with a 1D block of 256 threads.
    # Original stride = 256 → only thread 0 processes the first element, others skip.
    # Mutant stride = 1 → all threads process all elements, causing race conditions.
    input_tensor = torch.randn(16, 64, 1, 1, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    return [input_tensor]

```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `True`, original_ok: `True`, mutant_ok: `True`

#### Round 2 (未杀死)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the mutation changes the loop stride from `blockDim.x` to `blockDim.z`, but in typical 1D block launches, `blockDim.z` equals 1. However, the kernel appears to have additional constraints: (1) The loop upper bound `group_elements` is likely small (channels × spatial size per group), (2) With typical 256-thread blocks, original stride 256 means each thread processes at most 1 element when `group_elements ≤ 256`, while mutant stride 1 causes all threads to process all elements. This should cause massive race conditions and incorrect outputs, but the mutant survived previous tests. This suggests the kernel launch configuration might be different than assumed—perhaps using 3D blocks where `blockDim.z` equals the original `blockDim.x`, or the kernel contains hidden synchronization or thread masking that makes the mutant behavior equivalent. Alternatively, the normalization parameters (weight/bias) might be null, making writes idempotent (same value written by all threads).

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> Force a scenario where (a) `group_elements` is larger than the block's x-dimension but not divisible by it, (b) weight and bias are non-null to make writes non-idempotent, and (c) ensure the launch configuration is strictly 1D (blockDim.z=1). This will cause the mutant's stride-1 loop to process elements in a completely different pattern than the original, leading to incorrect indexing and wrong outputs. Use an odd spatial size to break alignment.

**LLM 建议的输入代码：**

```python
import torch
import math

def generate_inputs(device):
    # Use a non-power-of-two spatial size to avoid alignment
    # batch=1, channels=32, height=7, width=7
    # Group normalization with groups=8 -> 4 channels per group
    # group_elements = 4 * 7 * 7 = 196
    # Launch with 1D block of 128 threads (common but arbitrary)
    # weight and bias are non-null
    input_tensor = torch.randn(1, 32, 7, 7, device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
    # Return ONLY the input tensor as per specification
    return [input_tensor]

```

**GPU 验证结果：**

- ref_ok: `False`, original_ok: `False`, mutant_ok: `False`
- error: `ref crash: Expected weight to be a vector of size equal to the number of channels in input, but got weight of shape [64] and input of shape [1, 32, 7, 7]`

#### Round 3 (判定不可杀)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the mutation (blockDim.x → blockDim.z) doesn't change the kernel's output when the launch configuration uses a 1D block where blockDim.z = 1, which is typical. In this case, the mutant's loop stride becomes 1, causing every thread to process all group elements. However, because the computation for each output element is deterministic and independent of thread ID (same input, mean, inv_std, weight, bias per channel), all threads write identical values to each output index. Even with concurrent writes, the final value remains the same as with the original's partitioned work. The race condition is benign due to idempotent writes. Previous attempts failed because they assumed race conditions would produce different outputs, but the writes are idempotent when weight/bias are per-channel and the same across threads.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> The mutation is unkillable because the computation is deterministic and writes are idempotent. No input can produce different outputs between original and mutant as long as: (1) the kernel uses 1D blocks (blockDim.z = 1), (2) weight/bias are per-channel (or null), and (3) the kernel doesn't use thread-specific data in the computation. The mutant's different loop stride only changes work distribution, not final values.

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel lacks proper synchronization and thread-safety checks during reduction operations. The mutant reveals that concurrent writes to the same memory location can produce correct results by coincidence due to idempotent operations, but this is not guaranteed. The kernel should: 1) Use thread-private accumulators before reduction to prevent data races, 2) Implement proper warp-aware reduction for better performance and correctness, 3) Add assertions to verify thread block configuration matches kernel assumptions, 4) Ensure each thread processes distinct elements to avoid redundant work and potential write conflicts.


---

## 102. `L1_P35__mask_boundary__0`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P35`
- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L49
- **变异行**: Line 49, 原始片段 `sample_idx >= batch_size`, 节点类型 `ge>`
- **聚类标签**: Kernel Design Resilience

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx > batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutant survives because the boundary condition `sample_idx >= batch_size` (or `sample_idx > batch_size`) is never triggered under the standard kernel launch configuration. The kernel is launched with exactly `batch_size * num_groups` blocks, so `sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups` ranges from 0 to `batch_size - 1`. Consequently, `sample_idx` never equals `batch_size`, making both the original and mutant conditions false for all blocks. The mutation from `>=` to `>` only affects the case where `sample_idx == batch_size`, which never occurs, rendering the mutant behaviorally identical to the original for any input values given the fixed shape and launch parameters.

**LLM 最终判定**: 不可杀 (equivalent/unkillable)

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 1 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (判定不可杀)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the boundary condition `sample_idx >= batch_size` (or `sample_idx > batch_size`) is never triggered under the standard kernel launch configuration. The kernel is launched with exactly `batch_size * num_groups` blocks, so `sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups` ranges from 0 to `batch_size - 1`. Consequently, `sample_idx` never equals `batch_size`, making both the original and mutant conditions false for all blocks. The mutation from `>=` to `>` only affects the case where `sample_idx == batch_size`, which never occurs, rendering the mutant behaviorally identical to the original for any input values given the fixed shape and launch parameters.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> No input can expose the difference because the condition is dead code under the current kernel launch strategy. To kill the mutant, the kernel launch configuration must be changed to include extra blocks where `sample_idx == batch_size` (e.g., by launching with `grid_size > batch_size * num_groups`). However, the input shape alone does not control the grid size; it is determined by the caller's launch logic. Since the test suite only varies input values and not the launch configuration, the mutant cannot be distinguished.

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel contains a subtle vulnerability: it assumes the launch configuration always produces exactly batch_size × num_groups blocks, making boundary checks effectively dead code. This creates a robustness weakness because any future changes to the launch configuration could lead to out-of-bounds memory access without detection. Defensive GPU kernels should validate both block and thread indices against actual data dimensions, not just trust the launch parameters. Additionally, the thread reduction assumes a power-of-two block size without runtime validation, which could cause incorrect results if blockDim.x changes.


---

## 103. `L1_P35__mask_boundary__2`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P35`
- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L49
- **变异行**: Line 49, 原始片段 `group_idx >= num_groups`, 节点类型 `ge>`
- **聚类标签**: Kernel Design Resilience

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx > num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutation changes the boundary check from `group_idx >= num_groups` to `group_idx > num_groups`. However, `group_idx` is computed as `blockIdx.x % num_groups`, which guarantees `0 ≤ group_idx ≤ num_groups-1`. Therefore, `group_idx` can never equal `num_groups`, making both conditions equivalent (both are always false). The original guard was already redundant because the modulo operation prevents `group_idx` from reaching `num_groups`. The mutant survives because the modified condition doesn't change the program's behavior - both guards are dead code and never trigger.

**LLM 最终判定**: 不可杀 (equivalent/unkillable)

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 1 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (判定不可杀)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutation changes the boundary check from `group_idx >= num_groups` to `group_idx > num_groups`. However, `group_idx` is computed as `blockIdx.x % num_groups`, which guarantees `0 ≤ group_idx ≤ num_groups-1`. Therefore, `group_idx` can never equal `num_groups`, making both conditions equivalent (both are always false). The original guard was already redundant because the modulo operation prevents `group_idx` from reaching `num_groups`. The mutant survives because the modified condition doesn't change the program's behavior - both guards are dead code and never trigger.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> No input can expose a difference because the boundary condition is mathematically impossible to satisfy. The expression `blockIdx.x % num_groups` by definition yields values in the range [0, num_groups-1]. Thus, `group_idx` can never be ≥ num_groups (for the original) nor > num_groups (for the mutant). Changing the inequality operator doesn't affect which blocks are filtered out because no block ever has group_idx ≥ num_groups in the first place.

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel's boundary check `group_idx >= num_groups` is redundant because `group_idx` is computed as `blockIdx.x % num_groups`, which always yields values in [0, num_groups-1]. This creates dead code that can be mutated without affecting correctness, hiding potential logic errors. To improve robustness, the kernel should either remove redundant checks or replace them with meaningful validation of kernel launch parameters. Specifically, validate that the grid configuration matches expected bounds and that derived indices remain within valid ranges before array accesses.

## 104. `L1_P35__mask_boundary__3`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P35`
- **算子**: `mask_boundary` (Category B)
- **描述**: Weaken or tighten boundary checks in mask/tl.where (Triton) or thread/block guards (CUDA) @ L49
- **变异行**: Line 49, 原始片段 `group_idx >= num_groups`, 节点类型 `ge_rhs+1`
- **聚类标签**: Kernel Design Resilience

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 2: 变异后的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups + 1) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size
    int spatial_size = 1;
    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {
        spatial_size *= input.size(i);
    }
    
    auto channels_per_group = num_channels / num_groups;
    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
    
    // Configure kernel launch
    const int threads_per_block = 256;
    dim3 blocks(batch_size * num_groups);
    
    AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF(
        input.scalar_type(),
        "group_norm_forward_cuda",
        ([&] {
            group_norm_forward_kernel<scalar_t><<<blocks, threads_per_block>>>(
                input.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                output.data_ptr<scalar_t>(),
                weight.defined() ? weight.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                bias.defined() ? bias.data_ptr<float>() : nullptr,
                batch_size,
                num_channels,
                num_groups,
                channels_per_group,
                spatial_size,
                eps
            );
        })
    );
    
    cudaError_t err = cudaGetLastError();
    if (err != cudaSuccess) {
        throw std::runtime_error(cudaGetErrorString(err));
    }
    
    return output;
}
"""

group_norm_cpp_source = """
torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
);
"""

# Compile the inline CUDA code
group_norm_cuda = load_inline(
    name="group_norm_cuda",
    cpp_sources=group_norm_cpp_source,
    cuda_sources=group_norm_source,
    functions=["group_norm_forward_cuda"],
    verbose=False,
    extra_cflags=["-O3"],
    extra_ldflags=["-lcudart"],
    with_cuda=True,
)

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    """
    Optimized model that performs Group Normalization with custom CUDA kernel.
    """
    def __init__(self, num_features: int, num_groups: int):
        """
        Initializes the optimized GroupNorm layer.

        Args:
            num_features (int): Number of features in the input tensor.
            num_groups (int): Number of groups to divide the channels into.
        """
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.num_groups = num_groups
        self.num_features = num_features
        
        # Learnable parameters
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(num_features))
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(num_features))
        
        # Small epsilon for numerical stability
        self.eps = 1e-5
        
        # CUDA kernel
        self.group_norm_kernel = group_norm_cuda

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Applies optimized Group Normalization to the input tensor.

        Args:
            x (torch.Tensor): Input tensor of shape (batch_size, num_features, *).

        Returns:
            torch.Tensor: Output tensor with Group Normalization applied, same shape as input.
        """
        # Validate input
        if x.dim() < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected at least 2D input (got {x.dim()}D input)")
        
        if x.size(1) != self.num_features:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected input channels {self.num_features}, got {x.size(1)} channels"
            )
        
        if self.num_features % self.num_groups != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Number of channels ({self.num_features}) must be divisible by number of groups ({self.num_groups})"
            )
        
        # Use custom CUDA kernel
        return self.group_norm_kernel.group_norm_forward_cuda(
            x,
            self.weight,
            self.bias,
            self.num_groups,
            self.eps
        )
```

### Part 3: 未杀死该变异体的输入

**基础 get_inputs() 代码：**

```python
batch_size = 16
features = 64
dim1 = 256
dim2 = 256

def get_inputs():
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, features, dim1, dim2)
    return [x]
```

**LLM 最终存活原因**: The mutant survives because the kernel is launched with exactly batch_size * num_groups blocks, ensuring that blockIdx.x ranges from 0 to batch_size * num_groups - 1. Consequently, group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups is always less than num_groups, so the mutated guard condition (group_idx >= num_groups + 1) is never triggered—just like the original guard (group_idx >= num_groups). Since the guard is never true, the mutation does not affect execution; all blocks proceed normally, and no out-of-bounds access occurs. Changing input values alone cannot expose the difference because the guard condition depends solely on launch configuration, not on data values.

**LLM 最终判定**: 不可杀 (equivalent/unkillable)

### Part 4: 大模型诊断流程

共 1 轮迭代分析：

#### Round 1 (判定不可杀)

**LLM 存活原因分析：**

> The mutant survives because the kernel is launched with exactly batch_size * num_groups blocks, ensuring that blockIdx.x ranges from 0 to batch_size * num_groups - 1. Consequently, group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups is always less than num_groups, so the mutated guard condition (group_idx >= num_groups + 1) is never triggered—just like the original guard (group_idx >= num_groups). Since the guard is never true, the mutation does not affect execution; all blocks proceed normally, and no out-of-bounds access occurs. Changing input values alone cannot expose the difference because the guard condition depends solely on launch configuration, not on data values.

**LLM 杀死策略：**

> No input tensor (with any value distribution or shape) can kill this mutant because the critical factor is the kernel launch configuration, not the input data. The mutant only diverges if the grid size exceeds batch_size * num_groups, allowing a block with group_idx = num_groups to pass the guard. However, the launch configuration is fixed by the host code and is not part of the input. Without changing the grid size, the mutant remains behaviorally identical to the original.

#### 鲁棒性增强建议

> The kernel lacks defensive boundary validation for its launch configuration. While the kernel assumes it's launched with exactly batch_size * num_groups blocks, no runtime verification ensures this matches the actual grid dimensions. A robust kernel should validate that blockIdx.x doesn't exceed the maximum expected index given the problem size. Instead of relying on launch-time guarantees, the kernel should include a guard clause that checks both blockIdx.x AND computed indices against their respective bounds, ensuring correctness even if launch parameters are miscalculated or changed.


---

## 105. `L1_P35__relop_replace__7`

- **Kernel**: `L1_P35`
- **算子**: `relop_replace` (Category A)
- **描述**: Replace relational operators (<→<=, <=→<, >→>=, >=→>, ==→!=, !=→==) @ L49
- **变异行**: Line 49, 原始片段 `>=`, 节点类型 `cuda_GtE`
- **聚类标签**: Kernel Design Resilience

### Part 1: 未变异的完整源代码

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import load_inline

# Define optimized CUDA kernel for Group Normalization
group_norm_source = """
#include <torch/extension.h>
#include <cuda_runtime.h>
#include <cuda_fp16.h>
#include <math.h>

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float(T val) {
    return static_cast<float>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ float to_float<half>(half val) {
    return __half2float(val);
}

template<typename T>
__device__ __forceinline__ T from_float(float val) {
    return static_cast<T>(val);
}

template<>
__device__ __forceinline__ half from_float<half>(float val) {
    return __float2half(val);
}

template<typename T>
__global__ void group_norm_forward_kernel(
    const T* __restrict__ input,
    T* __restrict__ output,
    const float* __restrict__ weight,
    const float* __restrict__ bias,
    const int batch_size,
    const int num_channels,
    const int num_groups,
    const int channels_per_group,
    const int spatial_size,
    const float eps
) {
    // Each block handles one group in one sample
    const int group_idx = blockIdx.x % num_groups;
    const int sample_idx = blockIdx.x / num_groups;
    
    if (sample_idx >= batch_size || group_idx >= num_groups) return;
    
    const int group_start = group_idx * channels_per_group;
    const int group_elements = channels_per_group * spatial_size;
    
    // Shared memory for reductions
    __shared__ float shared_data[256];
    
    // First pass: compute mean
    float thread_sum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        thread_sum += to_float(input[input_idx]);
    }
    
    // Block reduction for sum
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float mean = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        mean = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        shared_data[0] = mean;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    mean = shared_data[0];
    
    // Second pass: compute variance
    float thread_sqsum = 0.0f;
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int input_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        float val = to_float(input[input_idx]);
        float diff = val - mean;
        thread_sqsum += diff * diff;
    }
    
    // Block reduction for variance
    shared_data[threadIdx.x] = thread_sqsum;
    __syncthreads();
    
    for (int stride = blockDim.x / 2; stride > 0; stride >>= 1) {
        if (threadIdx.x < stride) {
            shared_data[threadIdx.x] += shared_data[threadIdx.x + stride];
        }
        __syncthreads();
    }
    
    float inv_std = 0.0f;
    if (threadIdx.x == 0) {
        float var = shared_data[0] / group_elements;
        inv_std = rsqrtf(var + eps);
        shared_data[0] = inv_std;
    }
    __syncthreads();
    
    inv_std = shared_data[0];
    
    // Apply normalization with affine transformation
    for (int idx = threadIdx.x; idx < group_elements; idx += blockDim.x) {
        int c = group_start + (idx / spatial_size);
        int s = idx % spatial_size;
        int output_idx = ((sample_idx * num_channels + c) * spatial_size + s);
        
        float val = to_float(input[output_idx]);
        float normalized = (val - mean) * inv_std;
        
        if (weight && bias) {
            normalized = normalized * weight[c] + bias[c];
        }
        
        output[output_idx] = from_float<T>(normalized);
    }
}

torch::Tensor group_norm_forward_cuda(
    torch::Tensor input,
    torch::Tensor weight,
    torch::Tensor bias,
    int num_groups,
    float eps
) {
    auto batch_size = input.size(0);
    auto num_channels = input.size(1);
    
    // Calculate spatial size

    for (int i = 2; i < input.dim(); ++i) {

    auto output = torch::empty_like(input);
```
